claim: ETH is still likely to resolve above $2,200 on the governing April 17 12:00 ET Binance ETH/USDT 1-minute close, but the best post-synthesis estimate is modestly below the market because a single-minute, single-venue settlement should retain a few percentage points of tail risk even with spot still well above the strike.

frontmatter.contract_ambiguity_reason: rules cite Binance website candle while verification relied mainly on Binance API proxies
independently_verified_points: Binance ETHUSDT remained well above 2200 during synthesis-stage check (~2338)
Recent Binance 1m closes were still clustered above 2200
The market resolves on the Apr 17 12:00 ET Binance ETH/USDT 1m candle close, i.e. 16:00 UTC under EDT
frontmatter.verification_gap_summary: The main remaining gap is no direct near-resolution verification of the final Binance website settlement candle surface.
frontmatter.best_countercase_summary: A sharp overnight or late-morning crypto selloff or Binance-specific wick/anomaly could still flip the single settlement minute below 2200.
frontmatter.main_reason_for_disagreement: Remaining disagreement is mostly about how much tail risk to assign to a single-minute Binance settlement despite a ~6% cushion.
frontmatter.resolution_mechanics_summary: Yes resolves only if the Binance ETH/USDT 1-minute candle for Apr 17 12:00 ET closes strictly above 2200.
frontmatter.freshness_driver: A short-horizon crypto move before the Apr 17 12:00 ET / 16:00 UTC settlement minute can still change the outcome materially.
decision_blockers: No direct near-resolution check of the formal Binance website settlement surface
Single-minute path dependence leaves small but nontrivial tail risk
Little actionable edge remains versus a 97.5% market without fresher morning-of data
frontmatter.next_checkpoint: Recheck Binance ETH/USDT close to Apr 17 12:00 ET / 16:00 UTC if action timing permits.

sections.Alpha summary: Market implies 97.5% Yes; my synthesized range is 93% to 96% Yes. Directionally this still looks like a Yes market, but the edge is marginal-to-negative versus market rather than actionable. The only plausible mispricing is that the market may be a little too close to certainty for a one-minute Binance settlement.
sections.Input coverage: All five personas were available and usable: base-rate, catalyst-hunter, market-implied, risk-manager, and variant-view. Sidecars appeared broadly faithful to the raw findings; no major distortion was found. I also used bounded synthesis-stage verification with fresh Binance spot, 24h, and 1-minute kline checks. Coverage is complete because all planned personas were present and the core contract/mechanics evidence was repeatedly verified.
sections.Market-implied baseline: The synthesis baseline is the market-implied 0.975 Yes probability at the provided snapshot time. The swarm centered around roughly 0.95, already slightly below market, on the view that Yes is likely but extreme confidence is a bit rich for a single-minute crypto settlement.
sections.Syndicated probability estimate: Final post-synthesis estimate: 0.93 to 0.96 Yes. This preserves the swarm’s high-probability Yes view while keeping a real tail for short-horizon downside or venue-specific disturbance. I do not see support for moving above market confidence, and I do not see enough fresh contrary evidence to push materially below the swarm.
sections.Difference from swarm-implied center: This is not materially different from the swarm-implied center near 0.95. Fresh synthesis-stage checks slightly reinforced the swarm baseline rather than overturning it: ETHUSDT was still around 2338, the 24h low was still 2285.10, and recent 1-minute closes remained comfortably above 2200. So I stayed near the swarm center rather than compressing meaningfully toward the 0.975 market.
sections.Agreement or disagreement with market: I modestly disagree with the market on confidence, not direction. The market’s Yes lean is well supported by the current cushion above 2200 and the short remaining time. But 97.5% still looks a little aggressive for an unsettled contract that depends on one exact Binance minute close rather than a broader average or end-of-day level.
sections.Independent verification of edge: Verification quality is medium. I independently re-checked Binance ETHUSDT spot (~2338.37 during synthesis), Binance 24h stats showing a 2285.10 low, and recent 1-minute klines still closing above 2338. I also verified the operative timing logic: Apr 17 12:00 ET corresponds to 16:00 UTC under EDT. That is enough to confirm the core factual setup and that the swarm was not relying on stale levels. What remains weaker is direct verification of the formal Binance website chart surface very near settlement, so the small below-market view is only moderately independently verified, not highly verified.
sections.Compression toward market due to verific: No. The final range did not need meaningful compression toward market because the fresh synthesis-stage checks were consistent with the swarm’s baseline view that Yes is highly likely but not quite as certain as 97.5% implies. If anything, verification confirmed that the swarm’s slight discount to market was still defensible.
sections.Timing and catalyst posture: The key checkpoint is the Apr 17 12:00 ET / 16:00 UTC settlement minute. The main risk is not the absence of a bullish catalyst; it is a sudden downside shock before that minute. The edge, such as it is, is more likely to decay than widen without fresher pre-resolution data, because the market is already near the ceiling and only a sharp selloff would move the thesis materially.
sections.Decision blockers: There is no major contract blocker. The main blockers are practical: no direct near-resolution read on the formal Binance website settlement candle, residual one-minute path dependence, and minimal edge versus an already extreme market. These do not require new research to understand the trade, but they do argue for caution.
sections.Implication for the question: The best current synthesis is still Yes: ETH is more likely than not, and indeed highly likely, to be above 2200 on the relevant Binance noon-ET minute close. But the evidence supports high confidence, not certainty.
sections.Consensus across personas: All personas agreed that Yes is the likelier outcome. All converged on the same core mechanism: the contract is governed by Binance ETH/USDT, the relevant print is the Apr 17 12:00 ET 1-minute close, current price is materially above 2200, and the remaining No path is concentrated in a sharp short-horizon selloff or exchange-specific anomaly. All also agreed that this should be analyzed as a narrow settlement-mechanics question rather than a broad ETH thesis.
sections.Key disagreements across personas: The main disagreement was weighting-based / market-pricing based, not factual. Base-rate and catalyst-hunter were around 96%, risk-manager 95%, market-implied 94%, and variant-view 92%. The disagreement was about how much discount to assign for single-minute path risk and venue-specific fragility. There was only minor interpretive disagreement around UI-versus-API settlement-surface ambiguity, and no meaningful disagreement about the core contract wording.
sections.Best countercase: The best surviving countercase, represented most strongly by variant-view and partly by market-implied, is that a 97.5% market may still be too confident because one sharp overnight or late-morning move, liquidation cascade, or Binance-specific wick can decide a single-minute threshold contract even when spot remains broadly healthy.
sections.Encapsulated assumptions: Shared assumptions: Binance ETH/USDT remains the governing source; the noon ET candle maps to 16:00 UTC; ETH avoids a roughly 6% drawdown into the settlement minute; Binance trading/data remain orderly. Contested assumptions: whether the current cushion plus short horizon justifies confidence closer to 92% or closer to 96%. Fragile assumptions: that API-observed candles cleanly proxy the formal Binance website chart surface and that no venue-specific anomaly appears near settlement.
sections.Encapsulated evidence map: Strongest supporting evidence: repeated persona checks and fresh synthesis-stage checks all found Binance ETH/USDT materially above 2200; Binance 24h low remained above 2200; contract wording is explicit about venue, pair, timestamp, and close-price rule. Strongest contradictory evidence: crypto can move >5% in hours; one-minute resolution creates path dependence; a venue-specific wick or operational disturbance could matter. Authoritative source-of-truth evidence: Polymarket rule text plus Binance direct market-data surfaces. Mixed evidence: formal rules cite a Binance website/chart surface while verification relies mostly on API-accessible equivalents.
sections.Evidence weighting: I weighted most heavily the governing contract mechanics and direct Binance price evidence, including fresh synthesis-stage spot and kline checks. I downweighted generic crypto narrative speculation because the contract is near-dated and mechanically narrow. I largely ignored broad medium-term ETH theses because they add little to a <24h, exact-minute threshold market.
sections.Counterpoints / strongest disconfirming: The strongest disconfirming evidence is the mechanism itself: this is a single Binance 1-minute close, not a broad daily reference. ETH does not need to trend below 2200 for long to lose; it only needs the wrong minute to print below 2200. That makes sudden liquidation, macro headline risk, or venue-specific dislocation the real threat.
sections.Resolution or source-of-truth interpreta: Resolution mechanics are mostly clear. The contract resolves Yes only if the Binance ETH/USDT 1-minute candle for Apr 17 at 12:00 ET has a final close strictly above 2200. ET on that date is EDT, so the operative minute is 16:00 UTC. Minor ambiguity remains only because the rule names the Binance website/chart surface while most verification used Binance API outputs as a practical proxy.
sections.Why this could create or destroy alpha: Any alpha here is thin. If the market is slightly too confident about a narrow settlement mechanic, the value is in not overpaying for near-certainty. But because the market is already very close to the swarm and fresh verification did not uncover a material hidden risk, the likely alpha is small and fragile. The bigger mistake would be pretending a small residual tail does not exist at all.
sections.What would falsify this interpretation /: A material move toward 2250 or lower before the U.S. morning would reduce confidence quickly. A confirmed Binance-specific outage, abnormal wick behavior, or website/API divergence near settlement would also push the estimate lower. Conversely, if ETH remains comfortably above 2250-2300 into late morning ET with normal Binance operations, the view would move closer to the market ceiling.
sections.Highest-value next research: If anything is worth checking, it is a final Binance ETH/USDT spot and settlement-minute verification near Apr 17 12:00 ET / 16:00 UTC. Otherwise, none.
sections.Source-quality assessment: Primary source class: Polymarket contract rules and direct Binance market data. Most important secondary source class: Binance technical documentation for kline/timestamp interpretation. Evidence independence is medium at best because the core evidence necessarily concentrates around Binance, the governing venue. Source-of-truth ambiguity is low-to-medium, not because the rule is unclear, but because formal settlement references a website/chart surface while verification mostly uses API proxies. The synthesis is not meaningfully bottlenecked by missing personas, but it remains bottlenecked by the absence of a true near-resolution check.
sections.Verification impact: Yes, additional synthesis-stage verification was used. It materially confirmed that the swarm was not stale: Binance spot was still ~2338, the 24h low was still 2285.10, and recent 1-minute closes were still well above 2200. Cross-lane comparison also showed the disagreement was narrower than it first looked: nearly everyone agreed on facts and differed mainly on how much certainty discount to apply. I did not find a major lane inconsistency or provenance failure.
sections.Persona contribution map: base-rate — established the outside-view framing: current cushion plus short time to expiry makes Yes the default unless a ~6% drop or exchange anomaly occurs. catalyst-hunter — clarified that the key question is absence of a bearish near-term catalyst, not presence of a bullish one. market-implied — best articulated why the market is directionally right but perhaps a little too close to certainty. risk-manager — most clearly framed the residual risk as timestamp/path dependence and one-minute settlement fragility. variant-view — preserved the strongest below-market countercase that single-minute/venue-specific mechanics deserve a larger discount than spot-distance intuition suggests.
sections.Reusable lesson signals: Possible durable lesson: near-expiry crypto threshold markets are often better modeled as buffer-versus-settlement-fragility problems than broad directional research problems. Possible underbuilt driver: intraday path-risk / settlement-minute fragility may deserve explicit treatment across similar cases. Possible source-quality lesson: API checks are excellent for verification but should still be distinguished from the formal website/chart settlement surface. Reusability confidence: medium.
sections.Orchestrator review suggestions: review later for durable lesson: yes; review later for driver candidate: yes; review later for canon or linkage issue: yes; review later for swarm-method issue: no. Reason: this case repeatedly surfaced the same pattern that narrow exchange-settled markets hinge on settlement-surface mechanics and intraday path risk, which may deserve cleaner canonical handling and reusable process guidance.
sections.Recommended follow-up: Request decision-maker review only if action is still possible and sizing depends on whether a small below-market discount matters. Otherwise wait for the catalyst/settlement checkpoint; if revisiting, do one fresh Binance check close to noon ET rather than rerunning the whole swarm.
